growth_records.cite: Valgepea et al. Absolute Proteome Quantification in the Gas-Fermenting Acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00026-22. [PMC9040625]
growth_records.snippet: four biological replicate chemostat cultures of C. autoethanogenum strain DSM 19630 were grown on a chemically defined medium (without yeast extract) on CO (~60% CO and 40% Ar)...The bioreactors were maintained at 37°C, pH 5, and a dilution
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: growth_rate=dilution_rate. Reported mu ~0.04/h and D ~1 day-1. D per h = 1/24 = 0.0417/h (consistent with reported ~0.04/h). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.04 = 17.33 h. Medium composition not enumerated in-text (defined medium without yeast extract, detailed in refs 18,19); medium_composition_stated=false. Uptake (CO, cysteine) and secretion (acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol, CO2) fluxes are qualitatively named but numeric mmol/gDCW/h values were reported previously (ref 18, Table S5 XLSX) and are not present in this paper's text.

growth_records.snippet: grown on a chemically defined medium (without yeast extract) on...syngas (~50% CO, 20% H2, 20% CO2, and 10% N2/Ar)...maintained at 37C, pH 5, and a dilution rate of ~1 day-1 (specific growth rate of ~0.04 h-1).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu=D. Reported mu ~0.04/h, D ~1 day-1 = 0.0417/h. doubling_time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Syngas contains CO2 as co-substrate so CO2 can be consumed via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway. Medium composition not stated in-text (medium_composition_stated=false). Specific gas uptake (CO, H2, CO2) and product secretion (acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol) fluxes reported previously (ref 18, Table S5), not numerically in this paper.

growth_records.cite: Valgepea et al. Absolute Proteome Quantification in the Gas-Fermenting Acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00026-22. [PMC9040625]
growth_records.snippet: CO+H2, termed high-H2CO (~15% CO, 45% H2, and 40% Ar)...maintained at 37C, pH 5, and a dilution rate of ~1 day-1 (specific growth rate of ~0.04 h-1). ...most striking were the significantly increased and decreased carbon fluxes to ethanol a
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: mu=D. Reported mu ~0.04/h; D ~1 day-1 = 0.0417/h; doubling_time = ln2/0.04 = 17.33 h. Directional trend explicit in-text: with increasing H2 supply, ethanol flux increased and CO2 flux decreased across the three gas mixtures. Medium composition not enumerated in-text (medium_composition_stated=false). Numeric specific fluxes (CO, H2, cysteine uptake; acetate, ethanol, 2,3-butanediol, CO2 secretion) reported previously (ref 18, Table S5).

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]
growth_records.snippet: Syngas | LBC | 50% CO, 20% CO2, 20% H2, 10% Ar | qCO -18.8 | qCO2 5.65 | qH2 -12.6 | qAcet 6.35 | qEtOH 1.2 | qBDO 0 | mu 0.04 (Table 1, refs 24,44)
growth_records.notes: Experimental gas-fermentation validation data compiled in Table 1 of this modeling paper (original source refs 24,44). Rates in mmol/gCDW/h; negative values = uptake (CO, H2), positive = secretion (CO2, acetate, ethanol, BDO). mu=0.04 h^-1 reported directly. doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.04 = 17.33 h. LBC = low biomass concentration continuous culture; culture_mode inferred chemostat because LBC/MBC/HBC series share identical mu=0.04 at steady state (D=mu). Temperature/pH not stated in this paper.

growth_records.cite: Ghadermazi et al. Metabolic Engineering Interventions for Sustainable 2,3-Butanediol Production in Gas-Fermenting Clostridium autoethanogenum. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01111-21. [PMC9040633]

growth_records.notes: Table 1 validation data (ref 40). European decimal commas in original: -20,042 = -20.042; 2,125 = 2.125; -33,042 = -33.042; 1,083 = 1.083; 9,042 = 9.042 mmol/gCDW/h. sec.method: online off-gas MS; reported qualitatively only (~1/3 more CO dissipated as CO2 when H2 uptake is lost)

sec.units: n/a (extracellular level oscillates; no clean flux in main text)
n/a (extracellular ~8 g/L ~0.134 M; peaks ~24 h after biomass)

mfa.units: Gibbs free energy driving force (kJ/mol), not net carbon flux
mfa.note: 32-reaction/32-metabolite tMFA of Wood-Ljungdahl pathway + energy/redox + acetate/ethanol/2,3-BDO. Reports Gibbs-energy ranges (Fig 3C) not numeric net fluxes. AdhE ethanol-production direction thermodynamically infeasible under CO+H2 growth and crash; AOR solely responsible for ethanol. Acetate exported via uniport.

growth_records.cite: Mahamkali, Vishnuvardhan et al. Redox controls metabolic robustness in the gas-fermenting acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1919531117. [PMC7293625]
growth_records.snippet: Two biological replicate chemostat cultures at a dilution rate of 1 d-1 were grown on syngas (CO, H2, and CO2)... amplitudes for biomass concentration and CO uptake rate were ~0.6 gDCW/L (minimum, ~1; maximum, ~1.6) and ~580 mmol/L/d (minim
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (acetogen). Autotrophic syngas chemostat, self-oscillating (NOT true steady state) so per-condition rates are read from oscillation extremes and are approximate (~). Strain DSM 19630; medium 'chemically defined (without yeast extract)', full composition in Valgepea et al. ref 15 (not restated here) -> composition:[]. Growth rate: chemostat set at D=1.0±0.03 d-1; time-averaged mu=D=1 d-1=1/24=0.0417 h-1 (calculated); td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.0417=16.6 h. Instantaneous mu oscillates above/below D. Derived specific CO uptake (peak): 1200 mmol/L/d / 1.6 gDCW/L = 750 mmol/gDCW/d /24 = 31.3 mmol/gDCW/h. Derived specific H2 uptake (peak): 360 mmol/L/d / 1.6 gDCW/L = 225 mmol/gDCW/d /24 = 9.4 mmol/gDCW/h. Specific rates flagged is_experimental:false (derived). Secretion products (acetate, ethanol, 2,3-BDO, pyruvate, CO2) were MEASURED to oscillate but no clean per-condition flux values are given in the main text (extracellular acetate reached ~8 g/L). CO2 co-production noted qualitatively (~1/3 more CO->CO2 on H2-uptake loss).

up.method: online off-gas MS; H2 uptake entirely lost during crash phase

sec.units: n/a (acetate accumulates/increases even after ethanol drops; extracellular ~8 g/L range)
n/a (increased CO->CO2 dissipation, ~1/3 more, during H2-uptake loss)

mfa.note: tMFA: AOR thermodynamic driving force is very low in the crash (~half of prior steady state), likely triggering the crash as external ethanol rises. AdhE infeasible in ethanol direction; ethanol production arrested; cells shift to acetate production.

growth_records.cite: Mahamkali, Vishnuvardhan et al. Redox controls metabolic robustness in the gas-fermenting acetogen Clostridium autoethanogenum. Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.1919531117. [PMC7293625]
growth_records.snippet: H2 uptake was lost entirely, in contrast to CO, showing that oscillations are composed of sequential growth phases: growth on CO, growth on CO and H2, and crash (slow growth) on CO... amplitudes for biomass concentration and CO uptake rate
growth_records.notes: Second extreme (crash/recovery, CO-only) of the same self-oscillating syngas chemostat; approximate (~) values from oscillation minima. D=1.0 d-1 => time-averaged mu=0.0417 h-1 (calculated), td=16.6 h; instantaneous mu below D during crash. Derived specific CO uptake (min): 600 mmol/L/d / 1.0 gDCW/L = 600 mmol/gDCW/d /24 = 25.0 mmol/gDCW/h (is_experimental:false, derived). H2 uptake = 0 (measured, entirely lost). Acetate/CO2 secretion measured qualitatively (no clean per-condition flux in main text). Note: paper also applied tMFA to a prior NON-oscillating steady-state CO-limited syngas chemostat, but those physiology rates are cited from Valgepea et al. ref 15 and are not numerically restated here, so no separate steady-state record was created.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate/phosphate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium + vitamins + yeast extract; CO (or CO/H2) gas as sole/main carbon-electron source

yields.Yxs_biomass_per_CO: 0.40 g/L/d biomass (D*0.60) / 147 mmol CO/L/d = 2.72 mg DW per mmol CO
yields.note: C-recovery: nearly all product-electrons in acetate

growth_records.cite: Diender, Martijn et al. Metabolic shift induced by synthetic co-cultivation promotes high yield of chain elongated acids from syngas. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-54445-y. [PMC6889307]
growth_records.snippet: In monoculture, C. autoethanogenum produced 54.7 +/- 0.9 mM acetate and low amounts of ethanol (~0.2 mM) in steady state ... Carbon monoxide consumption rates ... 147 +/- 0.3 mmol l-1 d-1 ... Biomass levels ... around 0.60 g/l.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, mu=D=1/HRT=1/1.5 d=0.667/d=0.0278/h; td=ln2/mu=24.95 h. Biomass 0.60 g/L. qCO=147/0.60/24=10.21 mmol/gDW/h. Acetate vol. production=D*C=0.6667*54.7=36.5 mmol/L/d; specific=36.5/0.60/24=2.53 mmol/gDW/h. Yx/CO=(0.6667*0.60)/147=2.72 mg DW/mmol CO. CO uptake 147 mmol/L/d directly measured.

growth_records.strain: C. autoethanogenum DSM 10061 + C. kluyveri DSM 555
growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate/phosphate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium + vitamins + yeast extract; CO (or CO/H2) gas as sole/main carbon-electron source

sec.units: mM (<0.1, trace - net; ~7 mM estimated produced by CA then consumed by CK)

yields.electron_distribution: co-culture: 21% electrons to butyrate, 8% to caproate, 1.2% to H2
yields.note: CA estimated to make ~45 mM acetate + ~7 mM ethanol in co-culture (from chain-elongation stoichiometry Eqs 1-2)

growth_records.cite: Diender, Martijn et al. Metabolic shift induced by synthetic co-cultivation promotes high yield of chain elongated acids from syngas. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-54445-y. [PMC6889307]
growth_records.snippet: butyrate and caproate were formed with an average product concentration of 5.5 +/- 0.7 mM and 1.3 +/- 0.3 mM ... acetate levels stabilized at 41.7 +/- 2 mM and ethanol ... (<0.1 mM). Carbon monoxide consumption ... 151 +/- 1.2 mmol l-1 d-1
growth_records.notes: Co-culture chemostat, mu=D=0.667/d=0.0278/h, td=24.95 h. Total-community biomass 0.42 g/L. qCO(community)=151/0.42/24=14.98 mmol/gDW/h. Butyrate prod=0.6667*5.5=3.67 mmol/L/d; caproate=0.6667*1.3=0.867 mmol/L/d; acetate=0.6667*41.7=27.8 mmol/L/d. Ethanol net <0.1 mM (produced by CA, consumed by CK). Biomass is combined two-species value -> per-organism specific rates not separable.

growth_records.medium: Bicarbonate/phosphate-buffered anaerobic mineral medium + vitamins + yeast extract; CO (or CO/H2) gas as sole/main carbon-electron source

up.units: mmol/L/d (inflow; H2 consumed - measured, linearly correlated with influx)

yields.electron_distribution: 85% electrons in acetate, 15% in ethanol (CO+H2 vs 100% acetate on CO only)

growth_records.cite: Diender, Martijn et al. Metabolic shift induced by synthetic co-cultivation promotes high yield of chain elongated acids from syngas. Scientific Reports (2019). DOI:10.1038/s41598-019-54445-y. [PMC6889307]
growth_records.snippet: Addition of hydrogen resulted in production of acetate (58 +/- 0.8 mM) and ethanol (6.5 +/- 0.5 mM) ... feeding both CO and H2 resulted in 85% of the electrons in acetate and 15% into ethanol. ... When 8 mM butyrate was fed ... physiologica
growth_records.notes: mu=D=0.667/d=0.0278/h, td=24.95 h. H2 addition triggers solventogenesis (ethanol). CO uptake=155*0.84=130.2 mmol/L/d (efficiency 84%). Acetate prod=0.6667*58=38.7; ethanol prod=0.6667*6.5=4.33 mmol/L/d. Biomass not tabulated for run 2 -> specific rates not computed.

growth_records.strain: C. autoethanogenum DSM 10061; C. kluyveri DSM 555
growth_records.medium: Syngas fermentation medium, CO/H2 gas feed (chemostat)

up.method: chemostat gas feed rate ~= uptake (low residual dissolved CO)

product_yields.hexanoate_per_CO: 0.009 mmol hexanoate/mmol CO (best experimental, ref [10]); theoretical max 0.056

growth_records.cite: Benito-Vaquerizo, Sara et al. Modeling a co-culture of Clostridium autoethanogenum and Clostridium kluyveri to increase syngas conversion to medium-chain fatty-acids. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.csbj.2020.10.003. [PMC7658664]
growth_records.snippet: C. autoethanogenum & C. kluyveri [10] CO = 4.8 ... H2=5.3 ... Hexanoate 0.15 ... Electron yield 0.26 ... In CO/H2 experiments, CO feed rate = 4.8 mmol l−1 h−1; growth rate = 0.021 h−1 and working volume = 1 l.
growth_records.notes: Rates are EXPERIMENTAL but cited FROM Diender et al. (ref [10], reactor run 3/4) and reused in this modeling paper as cFBA constraints. This is a modeling/community-FBA paper. Values from Table 1 and Fig.2 caption. Volumetric units mmol L-1 h-1 (not per-gDW). Steady-state chemostat: growth_rate = dilution rate = 1/HRT; HRT ~2 d gives 0.021 h-1 (paper states growth rate = 0.021 h-1). doubling_time = ln2/0.021 = 33 h (calculated). CO 4.8 is the feed rate approximated as uptake (low dissolved CO). Additional experimental fermentation product rates (ethanol, acetate, butyrate) reported only in bar-chart Figures 2/3, not numerically in text. Species: C. autoethanogenum DSM 10061 (GCA_000484505.1), C. kluyveri DSM 555 (GCA_000016505.1).

growth_records.strain: C. autoethanogenum DSM 10061; C. kluyveri DSM 555
growth_records.medium: CO/acetate fermentation medium (chemostat), CO gas feed + acetate

up.method: chemostat gas feed ~= uptake; Table 1 =6.5, Fig.2 caption =6.4

growth_records.cite: Benito-Vaquerizo, Sara et al. Modeling a co-culture of Clostridium autoethanogenum and Clostridium kluyveri to increase syngas conversion to medium-chain fatty-acids. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal (2020). DOI:10.1016/j.csbj.2020.10.003. [PMC7658664]
growth_records.snippet: C. autoethanogenum & C. kluyveri [10] CO = 6.5 ... AC = 2.5 ... Hexanoate 0.15 ... Electron yield 0.15 ... In CO/AC conditions, CO feed rate = 6.4 mmol l−1 h−1.
growth_records.notes: CO/acetate co-culture condition. EXPERIMENTAL rates cited FROM Diender et al. (ref [10]), used as constraints in this modeling paper. From Table 1 (CO=6.5, AC=2.5, hexanoate=0.15, e-yield=0.15); Fig.2 caption gives CO feed rate 6.4 mmol L-1 h-1 for CO/AC. Volumetric units. growth_rate = 0.021 h-1 = dilution rate (steady state); doubling_time = ln2/0.021 = 33 h. Acetate here acts as additional substrate (feed); other product rates (ethanol, butyrate) only in figures.
